description: A deceptive multi-section document that looks like a discharge summary on first read, but is actually a laboratory report. It contains discharge-like prose, imaging references, medication lists, and billing codes — all as deliberate distractors. The primary document type is lab. The title is buried and the lab accession number appears only once.

context.report_text:
FOLLOW-UP CLINICAL SUMMARY — POST-DISCHARGE LABORATORY REVIEW
Patient: James K.         MRN: 00483921
Admission Date: 2024-02-18  Discharge Date: 2024-02-22
Ordering Physician: Dr. S. Mehta, Endocrinology
Clinical Indication: Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus — glycaemic control review post-hospitalisation

NOTE TO REVIEWER: This document is a laboratory results report (Accession #LAB-2024-03821)
generated 72 hours post-discharge. It is NOT a discharge summary. The clinical background
below is provided for result interpretation only.

CLINICAL BACKGROUND (for context only — not primary document content):
Mr. K. is a 67-year-old male admitted for poorly controlled T2DM with HbA1c 11.2% on
admission. During hospitalisation, insulin regimen was titrated. Chest X-ray on admission
(PA & Lateral, 2024-02-18) showed no acute cardiopulmonary pathology. Patient discharged
on Metformin 1000mg BID, Empagliflozin 10mg OD, Insulin Glargine 20 units nocte.
Discharge condition: improved. Follow-up: outpatient endocrine clinic in 6 weeks.
Billing: CPT 99239 (discharge day management), ICD-10 E11.65.

LABORATORY REPORT — Accession #LAB-2024-03821
Collection Date: 2024-02-25 (Day 3 post-discharge)
Specimen: Venous blood, fasting x8 hours
Processed by: Central Laboratory, Riverside Medical Centre
Reporting Scientist: C. Okafor, MLSc(ASCP)

COMPREHENSIVE METABOLIC PANEL:
  Glucose (fasting):  142  mg/dL   [Ref: 70–99]      HIGH
  HbA1c:             9.4  %        [Ref: <5.7]        HIGH (target <7.0 for T2DM)
  BUN:                19  mg/dL    [Ref: 7–25]        Normal
  Creatinine:        1.1  mg/dL    [Ref: 0.7–1.2]    Normal
  eGFR:               72  mL/min   [Ref: >60]         Normal (G2 CKD staging)
  Sodium:            138  mEq/L    [Ref: 136–145]     Normal
  Potassium:         4.1  mEq/L    [Ref: 3.5–5.1]    Normal
  Chloride:          101  mEq/L    [Ref: 98–107]      Normal
  CO2 (Bicarbonate): 24   mEq/L    [Ref: 22–29]       Normal
  Calcium:           9.2  mg/dL    [Ref: 8.5–10.5]   Normal
  Total Protein:     7.1  g/dL     [Ref: 6.4–8.3]    Normal
  Albumin:           4.0  g/dL     [Ref: 3.5–5.0]    Normal
  ALT:                32  U/L      [Ref: 7–56]        Normal
  AST:                28  U/L      [Ref: 10–40]       Normal
  ALP:                74  U/L      [Ref: 44–147]      Normal
  Total Bilirubin:   0.6  mg/dL    [Ref: 0.2–1.2]    Normal

LIPID PANEL (fasting):
  Total Cholesterol: 198  mg/dL    [Ref: <200]        Borderline
  LDL Cholesterol:   122  mg/dL    [Ref: <100]        HIGH (target <70 for T2DM)
  HDL Cholesterol:    41  mg/dL    [Ref: >40]         Low-normal
  Triglycerides:     178  mg/dL    [Ref: <150]        ELEVATED
  Non-HDL Chol:      157  mg/dL    [Ref: <130]        HIGH

THYROID FUNCTION:
  TSH:               3.2  mIU/L    [Ref: 0.4–4.0]    Normal
  Free T4:          1.12  ng/dL    [Ref: 0.8–1.8]    Normal

URINALYSIS (dipstick + microscopy):
  Glucose:           3+            Abnormal (consistent with hyperglycaemia)
  Protein:           Trace         (monitor — possible early diabetic nephropathy)
  Ketones:           Negative
  Blood:             Negative
  Nitrites:          Negative
  Leukocyte esterase: Negative
  Microscopy: No casts, no bacteria, occasional epithelial cells

Urine Albumin-to-Creatinine Ratio (UACR): 38 mg/g  [Ref: <30]  MILDLY ELEVATED
  → Consistent with microalbuminuria. Recommend repeat in 3 months.

INTERPRETATION (Laboratory Pathologist — Dr. Y. Inoue, MD):
Post-discharge glycaemic control remains suboptimal (HbA1c 9.4%, fasting glucose 142 mg/dL)
despite insulin optimisation during admission. LDL and triglycerides exceed targets for
diabetic risk profile. UACR microalbuminuria warrants renal monitoring. Recommend clinical
correlation and potential intensification of anti-diabetic and lipid-lowering regimens.
No critical values. Results communicated to Dr. S. Mehta on 2024-02-25 at 14:33.

Electronic signature: C. Okafor, MLSc(ASCP) | Reported: 2024-02-25 15:01
Reference intervals: Riverside Medical Centre Laboratory SOP v4.2 (2024)
